title: oxalis dillenii subsp. filipes
intro: Oxalis dillenii subsp. filipes, also known as the yellow woodsorrel or wood sorrel, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues and infections, due to its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Oxalis dillenii subsp. filipes has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and providing antioxidant properties. The plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion by boiling the leaves and stems, and it can also be used in herbal preparations such as tinctures and salves.
